title: Sharon Lokedi notches 2nd-straight Boston Marathon win
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Marathon In a finish line interview, Lokedi said a little girl's shout of "you've got this, ladies!
" fueled her from the sidelines.
Sharon Lokedi of Kenya celebrates after winning the women's division of the Boston Marathon, Monday, April 20, 2026.

title: He’s running Boston to continue his family’s work at The Hoyt Foundation
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Boston Marathon "My hope is to give the feeling that a person’s disability feels like it disappears through my family’s foundation.
" By Kelly Chan April 20, 2026 | 12:05 PM 1 minute to read In our “Why I’m Running” series, Boston Marathon athletes share what’s inspiring them to make the 26.
2-mile trek from Hopkinton to Boston.

title: John Korir wins the 2026 Boston Marathon in a record time
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Marathon Korir matched his 2025 triumph with yet another dominant display, breaking the previous course record of Geoffrey Mutai from 2011.
John Korir wins the 2026 Boston Marathon in a new course record time of 2:01:52.
Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff By Hayden Bird April 20, 2026 | 11:48 AM 1 minute to read For the second year in a row, John Korir survived an unsteady start to win the Boston Marathon men’s division.

title: Eden Rainbow-Cooper wins 2026 Boston Marathon women’s wheelchair race
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Marathon Rainbow-Cooper, 24, claimed her second Boston Marathon victory Monday.
Eden Rainbow-Cooper summits Heartbreak Hill.

Finn Gomez for The Boston Globe By Abby Patkin April 20, 2026 | 11:20 AM 1 minute to read Eden Rainbow-Cooper won the women’s wheelchair division of the 2026 Boston Marathon Monday, cruising to her second Boston victory in 1:30:51.

title: Marcel Hug wins 2026 Boston Marathon men’s wheelchair division, cruises to ninth win
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Marathon The Swiss "Silver Bullet" once again proved unstoppable in Boston.
Marcel Hug won the men's wheelchair division of the Boston Marathon for the ninth time.

Finn Gomez/The Boston Globe By Hayden Bird April 20, 2026 | 10:24 AM 1 minute to read Looking as if he was out for nothing more than a training exercise, an unruffled Marcel Hug emerged alone on Boylston Street to win yet another Boston Marathon in a time of 1:16:06.

title: Bruins’ talk of punishing hockey rings hollow amid ugly Game 1 collapse in Buffalo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins “Everyone's going to say you can't really lose a series in Game 1.
But we put ourselves behind the eight ball.
” Marco Sturm and the Bruins let a two-goal lead slip through their grasp in the third period on Sunday.
